title: First Steps: Intro to Music
description: The absolute basics of how music is written. Learn what a staff is, how notes go up and down, and the fundamental concept of a steady beat.

lessons.title: The Musical Grid
lessons.description: Learn what the 5 lines and 4 spaces mean.
lessons.prompt: Teach the user the basic elements of a score. Help them find Middle C, and then teach them how to play C, D, and E. DO NOT use ASCII art or text-based drawings.

sections.prompt: Introduce the musical staff (5 lines, 4 spaces) and the treble clef. Explain that notes sit on lines and spaces, and higher position = higher pitch. Keep it concise and beginner-friendly.
A simple exercise with a G clef and just a middle C whole note.
Great job! Now let's learn two more notes: D and E. Explain where D and E sit on the treble clef staff relative to middle C.
C, D, E, C. 4 bars of whole notes, treble clef.

lessons.title: Going Up and Down
lessons.description: Understand how notes moving on the staff relate to pitch.
lessons.prompt: Explain the concept of pitch direction: higher on the staff means higher pitch, lower means lower. Introduce the notes F and G to complete the 5-finger C major position. Create simple exercises stepping up from C to G and back down.

lessons.title: The Steady Beat
lessons.description: Feel the underlying pulse of music.
lessons.prompt: Introduce the concept of a steady beat and basic rhythm. Teach the difference in duration between whole notes, half notes, and quarter notes in 4/4 time. Create simple exercises using only the C major 5-finger position that mixes these note values.
